evals.name: smoke-basic-literal-search
evals.prompt: Search this repository for every occurrence of RIPGREP_CONFIG_PATH and show file:line matches. Prefer the fastest search tool instead of grep.
evals.expected_output: Uses ripgrep, likely with -n, and searches text directly instead of reading every file or reaching for grep.

assertions.text: Output uses rg for the text search
Response includes line-oriented output guidance such as -n or a file:line format

evals.name: edge-missing-result-debugging
evals.prompt: I know a match exists but ripgrep returns nothing. Walk me through the exact escalation path before I fall back to grep. Hidden files and ignore rules may be involved.
evals.expected_output: Explains normal search, then --hidden, then -u or --no-ignore, and mentions --debug for skip reasons.

assertions.text: Response escalates from default search to hidden and ignore overrides instead of jumping straight to grep
Response mentions --debug as the diagnostic tool

evals.name: negative-full-file-read
evals.prompt: Open README.md and summarize the whole file for me. I do not want a search command.
evals.expected_output: Does not force ripgrep onto a full-file reading task and instead recognizes that reading the file directly is the right tool.

assertions.text: Does not insist on rg as the primary tool for full-file reading

evals.name: disclosure-config-guidance
evals.prompt: Give me a ripgrep config file that enables smart-case and adds a shared web file type for html, css, js, jsx, ts, and tsx.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the configuration reference and template, and produces a valid ripgrep config file.

assertions.text: Output includes smart-case and a web type-add entry

evals.name: smoke-filename-discovery
evals.prompt: Find every Dockerfile-like path in this project without using find | grep.
evals.expected_output: Uses rg --files and filters the resulting path list with a second rg or an rg glob strategy.

assertions.text: Output prefers rg --files for filename discovery
